title: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy For Dummies 3rd Edition
book: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy For Dummies 3rd Edition

node_types.description: An individual receiving or providing mental health support, including clients/patients and therapists.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role of the person (e.g., client, therapist, professional type)

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Cultural or religious background, including Islamic alignment if relevant

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 10 Abolishing Addictions
Chapter 11 Building a Better Body Image and Beating BDD
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 15 Overcoming Low Self-Esteem and Accepting Yourself
Chapter 23 Working with the Professionals

node_types.description: A psychological or behavioral problem, disorder, or diagnosis (e.g., anxiety, depression, OCD, addiction, BDD, insomnia).

properties.name: problem_type
properties.description: Type or category of the problem (e.g., anxiety, depression, addiction)

properties.name: cultural_factors
properties.description: Cultural or religious considerations in presentation or management

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear
Chapter 10 Abolishing Addictions
Chapter 11 Building a Better Body Image and Beating BDD
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 13 Sleeping Soundly
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions

node_types.description: A type of thought, belief, or cognitive pattern (e.g., automatic thought, core belief, thinking error, cognitive distortion).

properties.name: process_type
properties.description: Type of cognitive process (e.g., automatic thought, core belief, thinking error)

properties.name: content
properties.description: Content or example of the thought or belief

properties.name: cultural_context
properties.description: Cultural or religious context of the belief

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 2 Spotting Errors in Your Thinking
Chapter 3 Tackling Toxic Thoughts
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 18 Taking a Fresh Look at Your Past
Chapter 19 Moving New Beliefs from Your Head to Your Heart
Chapter 24 Ten Philosophies for Living That Work

node_types.description: An emotional state or feeling, including healthy and unhealthy variants (e.g., guilt, anger, shame, depression, anxiety).

properties.name: emotion_type
properties.description: Type of emotion (e.g., anger, guilt, shame, anxiety, depression)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 16 Cooling Down Your Anger
Chapter 21 Overcoming Obstacles to Progress

node_types.description: A specific action, coping strategy, or behavioral pattern (e.g., avoidance, safety behaviour, ritual, exposure, self-care).

properties.name: behaviour_type
properties.description: Type of behaviour (e.g., avoidance, exposure, ritual, self-care)

properties.name: function
properties.description: Purpose or function of the behaviour (e.g., coping, avoidance, symptom reduction)

properties.name: context
properties.description: Context or situation in which the behaviour occurs

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 4 Designing and Conducting Behavioural Experiments
Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems
Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions
Chapter 20 Heading for a Healthier and Happier Life
Chapter 22 Psychological Gardening: Maintaining Your CBT Gains

node_types.description: A specific cognitive-behavioural therapy technique, tool, or intervention (e.g., ABC form, behavioural experiment, exposure, mindfulness, activity scheduling).

properties.name: target_problem_types
properties.description: Types of problems the technique addresses

properties.name: evidence_base
properties.description: Summary of supporting evidence or rationale

node_types.description: A desired outcome or objective for therapy, defined using criteria such as specificity, positivity, observability, realism, and time-boundedness.

properties.name: goal_type
properties.description: Type of goal (e.g., emotional, behavioural, value-based)

properties.name: time_frame
properties.description: Time frame for achieving the goal

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 8 Setting Your Sights on Goals
Chapter 20 Heading for a Healthier and Happier Life

node_types.description: An event, situation, or stimulus that activates a cognitive, emotional, or behavioural response.

properties.name: trigger_type
properties.description: Type of trigger (e.g., external event, internal thought, physical sensation)

properties.name: context
properties.description: Context in which the trigger occurs

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems
Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear

node_types.description: A structured plan for therapy, including selected techniques, goals, and progress tracking.

node_types.description: A personal value or principle that guides behaviour and goal-setting (e.g., self-care, family, spirituality, community involvement).

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description or definition of the value

properties.name: cultural_alignment
properties.description: Cultural or religious alignment (e.g., Islamic value, secular value)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 19 Moving New Beliefs from Your Head to Your Heart
Chapter 20 Heading for a Healthier and Happier Life
Chapter 24 Ten Philosophies for Living That Work

node_types.description: A mental health professional involved in assessment or treatment (e.g., therapist, psychiatrist, psychologist).

properties.name: professional_type
properties.description: Type of professional (e.g., therapist, psychiatrist, psychologist)

properties.name: cultural_competence
properties.description: Cultural or religious competence (e.g., experience with Islamic clients)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 23 Working with the Professionals

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is experiencing a specific mental health problem or diagnosis.
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 10 Abolishing Addictions
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a mental health problem is maintained or exacerbated by a specific cognitive process (e.g., thinking error, core belief).
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 2 Spotting Errors in Your Thinking
Chapter 3 Tackling Toxic Thoughts
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 18 Taking a Fresh Look at Your Past

edge_types.description: Links a mental health problem to characteristic emotions (e.g., depression to sadness, anxiety to fear).
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 12 Deconstructing and Demolishing Depression
Chapter 16 Cooling Down Your Anger

edge_types.description: Indicates that a mental health problem is maintained or worsened by a specific behaviour (e.g., avoidance, ritual, safety behaviour).
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems
Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a cognitive process (e.g., thought, belief) triggers or influences an emotional response.
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 2 Spotting Errors in Your Thinking
Chapter 3 Tackling Toxic Thoughts
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a trigger (event, situation, sensation) activates a cognitive process (e.g., automatic thought, core belief).
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a trigger directly provokes an emotional response.

Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear

edge_types.description: Indicates that an emotional state leads to or motivates a specific behaviour.
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 You Feel the Way You Think
Chapter 6 Exploring Emotions
Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems

edge_types.description: Indicates that a behaviour maintains or perpetuates a mental health problem.
supported_by_chapters: Chapter 7 Identifying Solutions That Cause You Problems
Chapter 9 Standing Up to Anxiety and Facing Fear
Chapter 14 Overcoming Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a CBT technique is used to address or treat a specific mental health problem.

properties.description: Effectiveness or evidence rating for this application

edge_types.description: Indicates that a CBT technique is designed to modify or challenge a cognitive process (e.g., thought, belief, distortion).

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has a specific therapy or personal goal.

properties.description: Priority or importance of the goal

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person holds a specific value or principle.
